# IL-42 Mediated CD8 Suppression

## Current View

IL-42 appears to suppress activated CD8 T-cell effector function without strongly blocking early activation. In the current source, the clearest signature is reduced granzyme B, with a weaker but still present drop in IFN-gamma.

## Key Claims

- [supports] IL-42 reduces granzyme B-positive activated primary CD8 T cells by about 38 percent at the tested high dose. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [supports] IL-42 lowers IFN-gamma-positive activated CD8 cells while leaving CD69 largely unchanged, which suggests preserved activation entry with reduced effector output. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [extends] The same suppressive trend is seen in a transwell setup, which is consistent with a contact-independent mechanism under the tested conditions. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]

## Contradictions and Caveats

- The conclusion is based on a single 24-hour time point.
- The receptor and downstream pathway are not identified.
- The contact-independent interpretation comes from one experimental configuration and needs replication.

## Open Questions

- Does IL-42 suppress CD8 function in vivo as well as in vitro?
- Is granzyme B the direct target of the pathway, or a downstream consequence?
- Which receptor complex mediates IL-42 signalling in activated CD8 cells?
